title: Illinois State Police Opens Criminal Investigation Into ICE Killing of Silverio Villegas González
excerpt: Illinois State Police launched a criminal investigation into the September 2025 fatal shooting of Silverio Villegas González by a federal immigration agent in the Chicago suburb of Franklin Park — eight months after the incident, triggered by the Illinois Accountability Commission's final report finding the shooting lacked apparent justification.
content:
On May 6, 2026, Illinois State Police announced the opening of a formal criminal investigation into the September 2025 fatal shooting of Silverio Villegas González, a Mexican national, by a federal immigration agent during Operation Midway Blitz in Franklin Park, Illinois.

  • The Illinois Accountability Commission's final report — released the same week — found "reasonable cause to believe that federal agents shot and killed Villegas González without apparent justification," citing DHS's own 2023 policy prohibiting agents from shooting solely to disable moving vehicles.
  • DHS had originally claimed an ICE agent was "dragged a significant distance" by Villegas González's car and suffered "severe injuries." However, body camera footage from the scene showed the same agent describing his injuries as "nothing major."
  • The FBI had been the primary investigating agency for eight months but made no public findings. Franklin Park police requested the state investigation after the Accountability Commission's report.
  • The ISP probe is the highest-profile independent law enforcement inquiry announced in direct response to the Midway Blitz operation.
"Our law enforcement officer followed his training, used appropriate force, and properly enforced the law," a DHS spokesperson said — a claim directly at odds with the commission's findings.

The case illustrates a documented pattern across multiple ICE shooting incidents: initial DHS claims of severe injuries to agents later contradicted by the agents' own statements on body camera footage.
mediaPlaceholder: Illinois Accountability Commission report excerpt finding no justification for the shooting of Silverio Villegas González
imageAlt: A photo of Silverio Villegas-Gonzalez sits at a memorial at West Grand Avenue and Emerson Street in suburban Franklin Park near the site where Villegas-Gonzalez was shot and killed by an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer during a traffic stop on Sept. 12.
